notes: Non-EU nationals generally need a recognised purpose to live in the Netherlands longer than short visits.
MVV entry visas apply in many (not all) long-stay procedures—confirm on IND and Netherlands Worldwide.

Some Dutch authorities require sworn translation—verify per procedure.

seo.title: Moving to the Netherlands from New Zealand | Visas, Documents, Costs & Practical Setup
seo.description: Planning a move from New Zealand to the Netherlands? Explore visa routes, MVV guidance, document prep, working holiday options, costs, and the practical steps to settle in.

move from new zealand to netherlands
relocating from new zealand to netherlands
moving from new zealand to holland
new zealanders living in the netherlands

faqOverrides.q: Do New Zealanders need a visa to live in the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: For stays longer than short visits, most New Zealanders need a Dutch residence permit for a recognised purpose such as work, study, family, or certain exchange routes. Whether you also need an MVV (entry visa) depends on your nationality and permit type—confirm on IND and Netherlands Worldwide.

faqOverrides.q: What is an MVV for the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: The MVV is a provisional residence permit used as an entry visa in many long-stay procedures. It can let you travel to the Netherlands to complete steps such as collecting your residence permit when your route requires it. Check IND pages and the New Zealand–specific Netherlands Worldwide guidance.

faqOverrides.q: Where do New Zealanders apply for the MVV sticker?
faqOverrides.a: Netherlands Worldwide describes how to apply for the MVV long-stay visa from New Zealand, including appointments. Follow the current country-specific pages.

faqOverrides.q: Can New Zealanders use a Dutch working holiday?
faqOverrides.a: Yes—New Zealanders can be eligible for the Netherlands Working Holiday Programme / working holiday residence permit under the conditions on Netherlands Worldwide and the IND. The IND explains how to apply when that route applies. It is not the same as standard long-term skilled employment.

faqOverrides.q: How should I prepare New Zealand documents for Dutch authorities?
faqOverrides.a: Govt.nz, MFAT, and DIA explain authentication, apostille, and official translation for documents used overseas. Dutch procedures may require legalisation and/or sworn translation—use the document list for your specific permit or municipality process.

faqOverrides.q: Where do New Zealanders find official support while in the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: MFAT publishes country information for the Netherlands, including travel advice and links to official New Zealand representation and consular topics.

customNotes: Long-haul moves from New Zealand often pair temporary housing, sea freight, and cross-border banking early.
